page: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 128.0 GB of RAM, which provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 614 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks position this server in the average tier across most standard workloads. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng score of 1900.09 and a multi-core score of 15530.37, while Geekbench multi-core performance reaches 10078.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 834.02 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 101.39 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database operations, the server achieves a PassMark database score of 7992.24 and handles 15.92 million Redis SET operations per second. For lightweight machine learning tasks, it demonstrates strong text generation performance of 289.27 tokens per second using a small 135M LLM model, though performance scales down to 12.36 tokens per second for a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E16ds_v5 offers a high density of memory relative to compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of higher vCPU tiers. The inclusion of 614 GB of local storage provides a built-in caching or temporary storage layer, reducing the immediate need for external disk allocations. This hardware profile is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and moderate web serving applications that require substantial memory capacity alongside moderate compute power.
description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage, with support for Premium Storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers balanced performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 10078.0 and a PassMark database score of 7992.24. It also demonstrates strong text generation capabilities on small LLM models, reaching 289.27 tokens per second. Lacking GPU resources, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require hardware acceleration. It is highly suitable for database operations, in-memory caching, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, substantial RAM capacity, and integrated local storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
614 GB of integrated local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong text generation performance of 289.27 tokens/sec on small LLM models
Optimized for database operations, in-memory caching, and software compilation
